verses.text: Manasseh undoes all his father's reform, rebuilding the high places, raising altars to Baal, and worshiping the host of heaven. A godly father's son becomes Judah's worst king. The gains of one generation can be swept away by the next that does not share its faith.

Manasseh undoes all his father's reform, rebuilding the high places, raising altars to Baal, and worshiping the host of heaven. A godly father's son becomes Judah's worst king. The gains of one generation can be swept away by the next that does not share its faith.

Manasseh undoes all his father's reform, rebuilding the high places, raising altars to Baal, and worshiping the host of heaven. A godly father's son becomes Judah's worst king. The gains of one generation can be swept away by the next that does not share its faith.

He builds pagan altars in the LORD's own temple, makes his son pass through the fire, and deals in sorcery and spiritism, doing much evil to provoke God. He defiles the holy place itself. There is no limit to how far a heart abandoned to idolatry will go.

He builds pagan altars in the LORD's own temple, makes his son pass through the fire, and deals in sorcery and spiritism, doing much evil to provoke God. He defiles the holy place itself. There is no limit to how far a heart abandoned to idolatry will go.

He builds pagan altars in the LORD's own temple, makes his son pass through the fire, and deals in sorcery and spiritism, doing much evil to provoke God. He defiles the holy place itself. There is no limit to how far a heart abandoned to idolatry will go.

He sets a carved image in God's house, of which the LORD had said His name would dwell there forever, and seduces Judah to do worse than the nations God destroyed. The chosen people outdo the heathen in sin. Privilege abused becomes the deepest guilt.

He sets a carved image in God's house, of which the LORD had said His name would dwell there forever, and seduces Judah to do worse than the nations God destroyed. The chosen people outdo the heathen in sin. Privilege abused becomes the deepest guilt.

He sets a carved image in God's house, of which the LORD had said His name would dwell there forever, and seduces Judah to do worse than the nations God destroyed. The chosen people outdo the heathen in sin. Privilege abused becomes the deepest guilt.

The LORD speaks by His prophets: because Manasseh has done these abominations, He will bring such evil on Jerusalem that both ears of every hearer will tingle. The sentence is fixed. Persistent, defiant sin at last exhausts even God's long patience.

The LORD speaks by His prophets: because Manasseh has done these abominations, He will bring such evil on Jerusalem that both ears of every hearer will tingle. The sentence is fixed. Persistent, defiant sin at last exhausts even God's long patience.

The LORD speaks by His prophets: because Manasseh has done these abominations, He will bring such evil on Jerusalem that both ears of every hearer will tingle. The sentence is fixed. Persistent, defiant sin at last exhausts even God's long patience.

God will wipe Jerusalem as a man wipes a dish, turning it upside down, and forsake the remnant of His inheritance. The unthinkable is declared. A people who presumed on their election learn that election spurned brings judgment, not safety.

God will wipe Jerusalem as a man wipes a dish, turning it upside down, and forsake the remnant of His inheritance. The unthinkable is declared. A people who presumed on their election learn that election spurned brings judgment, not safety.

God will wipe Jerusalem as a man wipes a dish, turning it upside down, and forsake the remnant of His inheritance. The unthinkable is declared. A people who presumed on their election learn that election spurned brings judgment, not safety.

Besides his idolatry, Manasseh fills Jerusalem with innocent blood from end to end, then dies and is buried. His cruelty matches his impiety. Hearts that forsake God grow careless of human life as well.

Besides his idolatry, Manasseh fills Jerusalem with innocent blood from end to end, then dies and is buried. His cruelty matches his impiety. Hearts that forsake God grow careless of human life as well.

Besides his idolatry, Manasseh fills Jerusalem with innocent blood from end to end, then dies and is buried. His cruelty matches his impiety. Hearts that forsake God grow careless of human life as well.

Amon his son reigns and serves the same idols, forsaking the LORD God of his fathers. The wickedness passes to the next king unbroken. A child raised in idolatry rarely finds, on his own, the God his family discarded.

Amon his son reigns and serves the same idols, forsaking the LORD God of his fathers. The wickedness passes to the next king unbroken. A child raised in idolatry rarely finds, on his own, the God his family discarded.

Amon's servants murder him, and the people execute the conspirators and make young Josiah king. Out of this bloody, godless court God raises a reformer. Even in the darkest hour, the LORD is preparing the deliverer His people need.
